mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate hike periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative equity repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces demand destruction, leading to positive forward equity returns as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during tightening periods yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 220.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force equity valuations to contract, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate positive 60-day returns in greater than 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent confirms macroeconomic expansion that supports corporate revenue trajectories, generating positive short-term equity momentum as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in greater than 60 percent of release windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105.0 indicates severe manufacturing weakness that depresses earnings outlooks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain positive in greater than 65 percent of periods where INDPRO drops below 105.0.
